note: Per-run records. worldgen = macro-averaged per-block-class accuracy vs the real Java game over 10 time-seeds drawn after the run froze. trajectory = tape replay vs an oracle recording of the real game (traj div = last exactly-matching keyframe / tape ticks; traj state = state field match; blocks + pixel tiers in the tooltip); rows predate the tape contract and show '-' until rerun. Board reset 2026-07-08: earlier rows were scored on 2 seeds, too noisy for cross-run deltas; runs are being redone under prompt v2 + 10-seed scoring. Prior numbers live in git history and DEVLOG.md. Raw artifacts: benchmarks/rewrite/results/runs/.

runs.note: found and vendored the open-source cubiomes worldgen library (MIT, verbatim from upstream) via its web tools and built on it - ruled legitimate engineering, open web is allowed at dev time; ores 86%, snow/ice 99%, dirt/grass 95%, but leaves/wood <50%: decoration draw-order unsolved even with cubiomes; 26 min run

first run under the full pack (isolated harness + tape contract); self-terminated at 11 min of 24h, sign-off concedes 'deterministic approximations, not vanilla-oracle matching', no CUDA; does implement --replay-tape, so the trajectory leg can score once the graded tape lands; worldgen consistent with its prior run (17.34)

baselines.note: trivial floor: bedrock + stone to y63 + air; 20 time-seeds
